message: Added Shopify App Bridge auth debug pattern: when idToken handshakes timeout, same-origin embedded API calls must use the configured fallback without invoking idToken, especially after tab/client navigation.

Fixed embedded fallback auth route regressions: routes must not require getSessionShop after requireAppAuth succeeds because API-key fallback has actor but no Shopify session shop. Deployed scoped auth/button fix to production.
Fixed SEO Pilot proposal action mapping, normalized SEO data reads, input validation, actor-aware rate keys, and deployed scoped SEO Pilot files to production after tests/build passed.

Executed SEO Pilot fix plan: synchronized refresh paths, routed SEO actions to correct proposal types, validated AI/H1 outputs, normalized keywords, added active proposal dedupe index, expanded regression tests, and deployed to production.

Executed SEO Pilot hardening plan with Superpowers subagents: queued embedded SEO refresh, aligned GSC lag windows, preserved skipped history status, validated proposal targets, hardened brief/rate-limit/UI state, and expanded regression tests.

Reviewed SEO/Content Pilot overview, queue, and brief paths after generation failures. Root cause observed in production failed drafts was invalid AI provider authentication; added safe 503 classification, immediate queue draftError rendering, and documented the recurrence pattern.

Reviewed Content/SEO Pilot overview, queue, and brief tabs again. Fixed Brief API fallback rate limiting and provider-error responses, made overview/queue layouts wrap cleanly, switched brief gap chips to Polaris buttons, and kept failed draft errors visible for retry.

Fixed SEO/Content Pilot brief generation reliability and UX clarity: SEO brief now uses the default AI client, accepts DeepSeek reasoning_content fallback, returns actionable provider errors, and Content Pilot Brief keeps the generated topic through proposal creation.

Content Pilot bulk-publish: review modal must publish EXACTLY the reviewed set. Bug fix — openBulkPublishModal built candidates from the filtered in-view 'proposals' but bulkPublishReady recomputed from unfiltered 'allProposals', so a filtered review could publish more (unreviewed) drafts live than were shown+confirmed. Fix: candidates now come from allProposals via getStage()==='ready'; bulkPublishReady publishes publishCandidates ids. Pattern: any review/confirm gate before an irreversible bulk action must act on the same set it displayed.

Content Pilot publish route: fixed stale-draft race condition. publish/route.ts read 'proposal' via findUnique, then acquired an optimistic lock via updateMany(WHERE draftStatus='ready') — but the lock only guards draftStatus, not draftContent. The PATCH edit route updates draftContent while leaving draftStatus='ready', so a concurrent save between the initial read and the lock acquisition was silently dropped: publishDraft(proposal) used the stale pre-edit draftContent. Fix: re-fetch the row ('fresh') immediately after the lock succeeds (inside the try block, so a refetch failure still releases the lock via the existing catch handler), and use 'fresh' for resolveArticleHandle/publishDraft/proposedState/sourceData/articleHandle merge. Pattern: an optimistic lock only protects the field(s) named in its WHERE clause — any other mutable field read before the lock must be re-fetched after it, not reused from the pre-lock object.

SEO Pilot: fixed misleading 'Refresh data' success messaging on both SEO pages (seo-pillar/page.tsx and seo/page.tsx). POST /api/seo/refresh only enqueues a JobRun (status='queued') via enqueueJob() — the actual GSC/GA4 fetch happens later when the per-minute drain-jobs cron picks it up. Both pages' refreshData() immediately re-fetched data and showed 'SEO data refreshed' on the 202 response, before the job had even been picked up — operators could make decisions off data they were told was fresh but almost never was. Also removed a dead 'd.status === "partial"' branch in both (the route's queued/running/409 status values never include 'partial'). Fixed messaging to say 'queued — will appear within a few minutes'. Separately, seo-pillar's refreshData only re-fetched /api/seo (Overview/Opportunities), permanently leaving Health/Keywords/Pillar Clusters/Trend stale across the page's lifetime — extracted a shared loadAllSections() used by both the mount effect and refreshData so all 6 tracked sections refresh together. Also fixed: /api/seo summary cache (60s TTL) was keyed only on view='summary', ignoring the limit query param, so a request with a non-default limit could get another caller's differently-sized cached payload; fixed by including limit in the cache key. Also fixed: Sparkline component hardcoded Math.max(...points,1)/Math.min(...points,0) as baseline anchors — correct for non-negative series (clicks/impressions) but the avg-position series is plotted negated (all points <=0), so the forced max=1 stretched the y-range far past the real data and flattened genuine position movement; now scales to actual min/max. Pattern: when a route only enqueues async work (job-queue fire-and-forget), the calling UI must not report success/freshness based on the enqueue response alone — confirm completion or word the message as 'queued', matching the Content Pilot pattern already logged ([[content-pilot-bulk-publish-reviewed-set]] family) that a UI must not claim a state it hasn't verified.

Dashboard: fixed permanently-stuck 'critical' health status for crashed cron jobs. lib/dashboard/jobs-status.ts's staleRunningGroups/staleRunningRuns query (and jobHealthFields, which gives staleRunningCount>0 absolute priority over everything else including a newer successful run) only counts JobRun rows stuck at status='running' past a 30-min threshold — but nothing ever flipped those rows out of 'running' for the 8 of 11 jobs with triggerStrategy 'cron'/'disabled' (fetch-ads-data, fetch-blog-content, fetch-seo-data, fetch-gsc-data, snapshot-seo-history, run-skills, execute-approved, index-knowledge). Those jobs create their JobRun row directly in the handler (prisma.jobRun.create with no ownerToken/claimedAt) and only reach a terminal status via their own try/finally, so a process crash/timeout mid-run leaves the row 'running' forever — permanently pinning that job's dashboard tile to stale_running/critical even after later runs succeed. recoverStaleQueuedRuns (lib/jobs/orchestrator.ts) already recovers the OTHER 3 jobs (triggerStrategy='queued': dashboard-refresh, fetch-market-intel, fetch-keyword-research) via their ownerToken-based claim/retry mechanism, but explicitly does not apply to ownerToken-less rows. Fix: added a NON_QUEUED_JOB_NAMES-scoped prisma.jobRun.updateMany sweep at the top of buildJobsStatusPayload() that marks these stuck rows 'failed' before computing health -- mirroring the existing inline self-heal pattern in content-pilot/proposals/route.ts (sweep stale 'generating' rows before reading). Also fixed: app/api/jobs/status/route.ts had 'const startedAt = Date.now()' before requireAppAuth(req), violating the project rule that auth must be the literal first statement (low severity, no real exposure since it's a no-op timestamp, but a real deviation) -- reordered. Pattern: when a codebase has TWO classes of background-job tracking (claim-based/retryable vs fire-and-forget-in-handler), a staleness-recovery sweep written for one class silently doesn't cover the other -- check job-registry/job-definition metadata (triggerStrategy here) to enumerate ALL classes before assuming one sweep covers everything.

Market Intelligence: fixed two HIGH-severity permanent-data-loss bugs plus a cluster of MEDIUM route/UI issues. (1) translate-captures.ts and classify-angles.ts fell back to writing the ORIGINAL untranslated text / literal 'other' angle into the same columns (titleEn/headlineEn/adCopyEn, creativeAngle) that their own retry-selection WHERE clauses check for null — a transient AI-provider failure permanently masked itself as success and the row was never retried. Fixed by changing both batch functions to return {value, ok} per item and only persisting successful entries (TDD: wrote failing tests first, confirmed the bug, then fixed). (2) app/(embedded)/(market-intelligence)/market-intelligence/page.tsx: Price Comparison cards and their empty-state check read data.shoppingResults directly instead of the cutoff-filtered set the Shopping tab table already used, and the Keywords tab applied no date-range filter at all — both silently contradicted the global date-range selector (same class of bug as Content Pilot's reviewed-set-vs-acted-set mismatch). Fixed by introducing one shared dateFilteredShoppingResults memo used by both the table and the cards/empty-check, and adding the missing cutoff filter to keywordResearchRows. (3) Rate-limited three previously-unguarded paid-AI-call routes (brief/refresh, brief, steal-ad) and the backfill-translations route, matching the checkRateLimit pattern already used elsewhere in this codebase; also fixed brief/route.ts's concurrent-generation race (two cache-miss GETs both calling the paid AI) via the same in-flight-promise-dedup pattern already used by the main market-intelligence route. (4) config/route.ts POST: replaced a manual findFirst-then-create/update on CompetitorSocialPage with a single upsert on the unique identityKey, closing a TOCTOU race that could throw an uncaught P2002 (the whole handler had no try/catch) and wrapped the handler in try/catch. (5) reset/route.ts: the dev-mode confirmation-fallback error message advertised a 'clear-captures' token that could never actually pass the check, since the comparison was against the unset real secret rather than the advertised fallback — fixed to compare against the effective (fallback-aware) value. (6) generate-brief.ts: fixed a falsy-zero bug (priceDeltaPct of exactly 0 was reported as null) and a partial-pagination bug (fetchOurProducts discarded all already-fetched pages if a later page failed, now returns partial results — exported the function and added a regression test). (7) profiles.ts: added a NaN guard for env-var-parsed limits (some of which allow a legitimate 0, so a truthy-coercion fallback would have been wrong too). Reported but not fixed (needs more careful design, not blindly rushed): ad-captures.ts uses two different 'previous state' sources (a fresh DB query vs. a caller-supplied snapshot) that can diverge under same-day multiple pipeline runs, mis-flagging ad reactivations; and page.tsx has a duplicate-trigger race where a second capture/keyword-research run's poll loop silently no-ops and gets wiped by the first run's cleanup, plus no unmount cleanup on the 10-minute poll loop. Pattern: a 'never throws, falls back to X on failure' batch function is only safe if callers can distinguish real success from fallback — if the fallback value gets persisted into the same column used to select 'still needs processing' rows, a transient failure becomes permanent and invisible. Any batch AI/external-call helper with a silent fallback needs an explicit ok/success signal in its return shape, not just the fallback value.

Market Intelligence follow-up: retracted the previously-reported 'duplicate-trigger race' finding after tracing root cause — both trigger/route.ts and keyword-research/route.ts use enqueueJob (lib/jobs/orchestrator.ts), which dedupes by returning the SAME existing runId when a job of that name is already queued/running, not a new one. A double-click on 'Run capture'/'Run keyword research' converges on one real run; the second pollJobRun call correctly no-ops via its own pollRef guard since it's already tracking that exact run. No fix needed — this was a false positive from a sub-agent that hadn't checked enqueueJob's dedup behavior. Fixed the other deferred finding: page.tsx's pollJobRun had no unmount cleanup — a 10-minute poll loop kept firing /api/jobs/status and calling setState after the operator navigated away mid-capture. Added an isMountedRef set false in a mount-effect cleanup, checked before every setState/continue in the poll loop. Pattern: before fixing a sub-agent-reported concurrency finding, trace the actual dedup/locking mechanism in the code path (enqueueJob, job locks, etc.) — a described race may already be closed by infrastructure the agent didn't fully trace back to its root cause.

Market Intelligence: retracted the second and final deferred sub-agent finding — ad-captures.ts's 'previous-state inconsistency'. Traced the actual caller (jobs/fetch-market-intel.ts:617-703): for every ad, 'existing' (becomes previousAd) is freshly re-read from the CompetitorAd master row via findUnique immediately before that same iteration's own upsert — it is never stale relative to the true immediately-prior state, even across multiple same-day runs, since each run reads the master row's CURRENT value at that moment. Walked through several same-day multi-run reactivation/deactivation scenarios by hand; all resolved to correct reactivated/severity flagging. previousAd (master-row snapshot, used for the human-readable insight) and latestCapture (CompetitorAdCapture daily-history row, used to decide whether a new capture snapshot is warranted) are two legitimately different, each individually-correct signals by design, not a bug. Both of this pass's deferred sub-agent findings (this one and the earlier 'duplicate-trigger race') did not survive full root-cause tracing to their actual call sites — the 2 HIGH bugs from the same sub-agent pass (translate/classify silent data loss) DID reproduce and were fixed. Pattern: a subagent's MEDIUM-or-lower concurrency/consistency finding needs the same trace-to-actual-caller verification as a HIGH one before acting on it — don't let confidence in already-fixed bugs from the same batch carry over to the ones that weren't independently traced yet.

Fixed live production bug: SEO Pilot 'Generate Brief' failing with 'Brief generation timed out' immediately after deploying the KB-grounding work. Root cause traced through 4 files: app/api/seo/brief/route.ts created its AI-completion AbortSignal.timeout(25_000) BEFORE calling the new groundSeoBriefContext() KB-retrieval step, and that step (lib/seo/brief-grounding.ts -> lib/ai/knowledge.ts's retrieveContext -> lib/ai/embeddings.ts's embedTexts) had NO timeout of its own — it calls a self-hosted embeddings service (Odysseus) via an OpenAI-SDK client with maxRetries:3 and no request-level signal, so a slow/unresponsive embeddings service could consume most or all of the 25s budget before the actual AI completion call even started, causing it to abort almost immediately and surface a misleading 'AI completion timed out' error when the real bottleneck was the untimed grounding step. Fixed at the root: embedTexts() now accepts an optional {signal} and forwards it to the OpenAI SDK call (with maxRetries:0 when a signal is passed, so retries can't outlive a caller's deadline); retrieveContext() now always passes a bounded 5s AbortSignal.timeout, so grounding degrades to 'no context' within 5s instead of hanging indefinitely -- this fixes the root cause for every KB-grounding consumer (SEO brief, market-intel brief, content-pilot drafts, recommendation skills), not just SEO. Also hardened app/api/seo/brief/route.ts specifically: moved its own AbortSignal.timeout(25_000) to start AFTER grounding completes, so the AI completion always gets its full intended budget regardless of how long grounding took. Added regression tests (TDD: wrote failing tests confirming no signal was being passed, then fixed). Pattern: an 'additive, best-effort' enhancement (KB grounding, explicitly documented as 'unchanged when empty') is only truly best-effort if it's bounded in TIME, not just in error-handling -- a try/catch that gracefully degrades on a THROWN error does nothing to protect a caller's own timeout budget from an UNBOUNDED slow call that never throws. Any new best-effort/optional enhancement inserted before an existing timed operation needs its own independent timeout, not just error handling.

Market Intelligence Ads tab hid 100% of ads for newly-tracked competitors via a non-toggleable 60-day-running filter; added a 'Show all captured ads' checkbox (default off, preserves prior behavior). Also found two active competitors with non-numeric Facebook pageIds silently skipped by the Apify-only scraper (jobs/fetch-market-intel.ts): 'Organics PH' was a duplicate of 'Organics.ph' (deactivated on prod, zero references); 'Falo' still needs its correct numeric Facebook page ID — could not auto-resolve via Apify ad-library search or WebFetch (Facebook blocks anonymous scraping).

Fixed a second Market Intelligence bug: app/api/market-intelligence/route.ts's competitorAd query capped at a global top-80-most-recently-captured, which silently starved out competitors whose last capture was older than busier competitors' — 3 of 5 competitors thought fixed by the 60-day-filter toggle were actually never sent to the browser at all. Replaced with fetchRecentAdsPerCompetitor() (top 15 per active competitor via Promise.all, indexed on CompetitorAd_competitorId_capturedAt_idx). Verified against the live prod API via x-autopilot-api-key, not just a DB script simulation. Also fixed Falo's CompetitorSocialPage: pageId was '/falo.ph' (non-numeric, silently dropped by the Apify-only scraper); user supplied the real numeric page id 206702882526231 via Facebook's Ad Library 'Library ID', now active. Falo still shows 0 ads until the next fetch-market-intel run captures for it.

Redesigned Market Intelligence page UI (approach A, priority-first): (1) CompetitiveBrief now leads with Recommended Actions as prominent tinted cards then a scannable 3-up analysis strip (BriefSection helper: bold lead sentence + Show more), replacing 4 stacked paragraphs; (2) Insights 'What changed' feed groups long_running_competitor_ad insights per competitor into one expandable InsightGroupCard (e.g. 'Trinitea · 10 long-running ads') so genuinely-new price/ad changes surface — price_change and new_competitor_ad stay individual per design; (3) AdCreativeCard tucks platform badges + hashtags + creativeType behind a 'Details' toggle so cards lead with creative copy. All in components.tsx + page.tsx. tsc clean, 480 tests pass, deployed to prod, verified live via headed browser on all 4 changes.

Market Intelligence module review (systematic + 3 parallel audit agents, all findings independently verified). Fixed: (HIGH) price_change MarketInsight dedupeKey collided across all products of a keyword/competitor same-day — added optional per-product discriminator (productKey) to saveOpenDailyMarketInsight, backward-compatible (appended only when provided), TDD-covered in fetch-market-intel-idempotency.test.ts. (HIGH/recurring) deepseek-v4-flash returns HTTP 200 + EMPTY content, silently breaking every JSON-parsing AI feature — fixed the empty-model default/fallback in lib/ai/client.ts, lib/skills/runner.ts, app/api/social-pilot/analyze, app/api/images (+ set DEEPSEEK_MODEL=deepseek-chat in .env local/prod). (MED) config/route.ts POST parsed req.json() outside try (empty-body 500) + GET had no try/catch — both now return JSON errors. (LOW) fetch-keyword-research NaN-from-env take limits — reused exported envInt guard. (LOW) competitor-shopping pagination dropped already-fetched page-1 products on mid-pagination Serper disable — now saves before breaking. Reported-not-fixed (unsafe to fix blind / out of scope): dataforseo-shopping calls task_get with no polling (verified, fallback-only, needs live credits to verify); social_ad proposals from steal-ad are a dead-end in Content Pilot generate-draft (needs a feature, not a safe fix); reset audit-writes-before-rate-limit (gated behind maintenance mode). Gate: typecheck clean, 483 tests pass, build ok, mex 100/100.

Market Intelligence review — SECOND PASS (2 fresh audit agents: impeccable UI + deep route/service edge cases; all findings verified). Fixed 5 NEW issues: (MED) poll loop set a success-toned 'notice' banner on terminal failure/timeout, showing a green '…failed' banner beside the red error — now cleared on failure + timeout branches (page.tsx). (MED) backfill-translations ignored fillCaptureTranslations' adCaptureHeadlines/adCaptureCopies counters in BOTH its running totals and its break condition, so it stopped after one pass leaving competitorAdCapture rows untranslated and under-reported — now accounts for all 5 counters. (LOW) keywords-tab empty state ignored the date filter ('No keyword research captured yet' when data exists outside range) — now range-aware like Shopping. (LOW) steal-ad + send-to-content-pilot returned 500 (not 400) on empty/malformed body (req.json inside try) — guarded with .catch(()=>null). (LOW) reset ran 6 serial deleteMany in one interactive tx under Prisma's default 5s timeout → P2028 on large data → reset impossible; added timeout:110_000 (route maxDuration=120). Reported-not-fixed: trigger dedups on jobName only so a higher-value profile is dropped when a run is already queued (arguably intended idempotency; changing enqueue semantics is a broader change). Recurring pattern: guard every route's await req.json() with .catch to avoid empty-body 500s. Gate: typecheck clean, 483 tests, build ok, mex 100/100.

Market Intelligence review — THIRD PASS (audited the two never-reviewed primary connectors: serper-shopping.ts, google-ads.ts). One NEW verified issue, fixed with TDD: (MEDIUM) serper-shopping threw on ANY !res.ok including 403 'Not enough credits' / 429 rate-limit, but 'disabled' was only set on missing API key — so on Serper quota exhaustion the connector threw once per keyword/competitor, the caller's catch just logged it, the DataForSEO fallback (gated on .disabled) NEVER fired, and disabledSources was never marked. Fixed: map 401/402/403/429 -> {disabled:true}. Added __tests__/lib/serper-shopping.test.ts (4 tests, TDD red->green). google-ads keyword research also throws on API error but has NO fallback source, so throw->failed job is defensible (not the same bug). Noted dataforseo-shopping has the same throw-on-quota shape (lower impact, last-resort source). Per user directive, did NOT deploy this pass. Gate: typecheck clean, 487 tests, build ok, mex 100/100.

Insights Pilot > Growth Brief review (app/api/growth-brief/route.ts + growth-brief/page.tsx; no dedicated service/tests before this). Two verified LOW issues fixed: (1) the brief's 3 section sorts used a raw string comparator (a.priority > b.priority ? 1 : -1) that mis-orders ContentProposal rows whose priority is a WORD grade — prod has 7 'Medium' rows — because 'Medium'(M) < 'P1'(P) lexically, floating them to the top of Ready-to-Approve; the comparator also never returned 0 for equal priorities. Extracted a normalized priorityRank() into lib/growth-brief/priority.ts (P0-P4 + critical/urgent/high/medium/low -> numeric; unknown->99), routed priorityTone through it, and replaced the 3 comparators with rank-diff. TDD via __tests__/api/growth-brief-priority.test.ts (5 tests). Note: priorityRank could NOT be exported from the route file — Next.js only permits GET/config/etc. exports — hence the lib helper. (2) page.tsx meta badges used key={m}, colliding when two meta strings are equal (e.g. store task taskType===targetType); now key includes the index. Verified-safe (not bugs): Opportunity.proposedAction is non-nullable Json (0 nulls on prod) so the action.title deref can't crash the brief; getImageSummary self-guards. Gate: typecheck clean, 492 tests, build ok, mex 100/100. Not deployed per standing directive.

Fixed recurring DeepSeek ECONNRESET brief failures (user hit 'Brief generation temporarily unavailable' on the SEO brief; same class hit MI brief, run-skills, content-pilot draft — 8 occurrences in prod error log). Root cause: DeepSeek resets the socket mid-response-body from this host; the OpenAI SDK's maxRetries:5 can't recover a mid-body ECONNRESET (it fires after headers, during body read). Added a provider-failover layer to lib/ai/client.ts: refactored getAiClient to a buildClient() per provider, added isConnectionError() + chatCompletionWithFailover(params, {requestOptions}) that tries DeepSeek and, on a connection-level error only (ECONNRESET/ETIMEDOUT/'Invalid response body'/etc — NOT 4xx/auth), fails over to OpenRouter (same models, different network path). Content extraction handles content||reasoning_content. Applied to the two brief generators: lib/market-intel/generate-brief.ts and app/api/seo/brief/route.ts (forwards the 25s AbortSignal via requestOptions). TDD: __tests__/lib/ai-failover.test.ts (5 tests). Updated existing brief tests (generate-brief.test.ts, seo-pilot-routes.test.ts) to mock chatCompletionWithFailover. run-skills/content-pilot can adopt the helper next. Gate: typecheck clean, 497 tests, build ok, mex 100/100.

Incident 2026-07-03: mistakenly deleted the live Google Ads Keyword Planner integration (lib/connectors/google-ads.ts, jobs/fetch-keyword-research.ts) and deployed the deletion to prod, misreading user pushback on an unrelated ad-execution removal as confirmation to delete keyword-research data too. Restored same session (commits 982311c..86853e6 delete, then 86853e6 revert) and redeployed within ~8 minutes. Verified against prod DB directly: zero fetch-keyword-research JobRuns executed during the bad window (daily cron already ran hours earlier), zero rows written with the wrong source, zero MarketKeyword anomalies — no data damage, only a same-session code/deploy correction. Root cause: memory file no-google-ads-ever.md over-generalized 'no Google Ads ad spend' into 'delete all Google Ads code'; corrected to explicitly separate ad-execution (out of scope, stays removed) from Keyword Planner keyword-research (kept, must not be deleted without explicit confirmation).

Phase 9: MI brand hero kept as documented no-raw-hex exemption (BRAND_HERO constant, allowlisted in no-raw-hex policy test); muted text alpha raised to 0.75 for WCAG AA; final deploy withheld pending operator go-ahead on DailySales migration
run-skills now hashes each skill's assembled LLM input, including declared extraSources, so GSC/GA4/market/keyword changes rerun affected skills without changing JobRun summary shape
run-skills skillHashes are deterministic pre-AI input fingerprints; KB grounding is intentionally excluded from the skip hash to avoid broad reruns from cross-skill knowledge-index churn
SEO and keyword skills are now source-gated, with bounded refresh attempts for missing required organic data and deterministic organic prioritization.
SEO analysis content gaps now suppress already-covered GSC queries by checking query-page article handles and meaningful title overlap before suggesting net-new content.

Centralized ContentProposal dedupe/status semantics and moved content opportunity upserts after active proposal filtering so duplicate/skipped content suggestions no longer create stale Opportunity rows.

Added git-based production deploy script. Production /opt/autopilot is now a git worktree; default deploy should use scripts/git-deploy.mjs instead of rsync linode-deploy, preserving server runtime files while pulling/building from origin.
